lesson.menu_desc: Structuring the deal and presenting it to the board -- 27 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 10 -- Block 2 -- Deal Structure & Synergies
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Carlos Vinicius Vale Bassan | Aula 10 -- Deal Structure & Synergies

listenings.text: Carlos, before you present this deal to the board, let me brief you on how to frame it. Open with the strategic rationale: why this deal makes sense, not just the numbers. Lead the board through the story. Then show the valuation, and make it clear the price is fair. Most of your value comes from synergies, so explain them in plain terms: lower costs and a wider market. Confirm that you have completed due diligence and that the company is clean. The board will focus on two things: the equity split, how much we own after the deal, and the risk. So be ready for hard questions there. Remember, every stakeholder reads this differently, so keep one clear summary. Lead with the why, prove it with the numbers, and let the board decide.

Hi Carlos, this is Diana from the board. Thank you for a very clear presentation today. I wanted to leave you a short recap of where we landed. The board has approved the structure in principle, so the merger can move forward to the next stage. What we liked most were the synergies; the cost savings and the wider market made the rationale strong. The due diligence looks solid, and nobody raised a red flag there. Two points are still open. First, the board wants more detail on the equity split, exactly how much we control after the deal. Second, one stakeholder has a concern about timing that we should address. Please send me the final structure and the updated valuation, and we will hold the final vote next week. Great work, and talk soon.
